articles.title: Tom Aspinall Has a Bigger Problem Than Gable Steveson...
articles.description: Tyron Woodley says Gable Steveson would “torture” Tom Aspinall. But according to Chael Sonnen, that is not the real story. The real problem is that people are even asking the question. In this breakdown, Chael explains why this has nothing to do with whether Gable could actually beat Tom. The fight game does not work that way. It runs on perception. And right now, a fighter who has never stepped into the UFC Octagon is creating headlines over the heavyweight champion. That is a problem. Chael connects it to past moments in MMA, when rising names like Khamzat Chimaev were being compared to established champions like Kamaru Usman before they had even earned their spot. The difference here is what it says about Tom Aspinall’s current position. Because silence matters. If the champion is not controlling the narrative, someone else will. And when a newcomer can enter the conversation and take that spotlight without ever fighting, it signals a shift in how the audience sees the top of the division. This is not about who wins a hypothetical fight. It is about who the audience believes would win. And in this sport, that belief is everything. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that shape the division. #UFC #MMA #TomAspinall #GableSteveson #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Question Everyone’s Asking0:45 This Isn’t About Gable vs Tom2:00 How This Happens in MMA3:15 Perception Is Reality4:30 Tom’s Silence Is a Problem6:00 He’s Getting Out-Headlined7:30 The Champion Is Being Questioned9:00 This Is the Real Story Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Ronda Rousey Declares War on UFC
articles.description: Ronda Rousey didn’t just promote a fight. She reframed the entire game. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to Ronda stepping in as the face of MVP and turning her matchup with Gina Carano into something much bigger than a fight. This is no longer just Ronda vs Gina. It is being positioned as MVP vs the UFC. And that is where it gets interesting. Ronda is not selling punches, rounds, or even a clear set of stakes. She is selling a narrative. A brand battle. A sense that MVP is coming for the UFC’s territory. But as Chael points out, it does not quite line up with reality. There is no true head to head competition, no direct way to measure who is winning, and no traditional structure behind the matchup itself. Still, it is working. Chael breaks down why this version of Ronda might be the most effective she has ever been in media, how she created intrigue without the usual fight details, and why the MVP vs UFC angle feels compelling even if it is more perception than reality. Because in today’s fight game, controlling the narrative can matter just as much as the fight itself. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that do not follow the old rules. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Ronda Takes Over MVP 0:30 The “Market Share” Moment 1:00 This Promotion Makes No Sense 2:00 Selling a Fight Without the Fight 3:00 Why It Still Works 4:30 The Missing Weight Class Problem 6:00 MVP vs UFC Isn’t Real 7:30 This Was Never About Gina 9:00 Ronda Changed the Game Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Greatest UFC Champion in UFC History
articles.description: The greatest fighter of all time and the greatest champion of all time are not the same thing. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen explains why the GOAT debate has been missing a key distinction. The idea of the greatest fighter is subjective. T here is no tournament, no system, and no way to prove it definitively. But the greatest champion is different. Chael makes the case that Israel Adesanya stands alone when it comes to controlling a title reign. From hand-picking opponents to calling them out in real time, Adesanya dictated every step of his championship run in a way that almost never happens in the sport. While most fighters wait for the press conference, Adesanya made his moves in the moment. In the cage. In front of the audience. He turned every appearance into an opportunity to shape what came next. Chael breaks down how that approach allowed Adesanya to control his career, create interest, and maximize every phase of being a champion, from walkout to performance to post-fight. Because being the best fighter is one thing. Being the best champion is something else entirely. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the debates that never go away. #UFC #MMA #IsraelAdesanya #ChaelSonnen #GOAT 0:00 GOAT Fighter vs GOAT Champion0:45 Why GOAT Is Just Opinion1:30 The Case for Adesanya2:30 How He Controlled His Opponents3:45 Calling Shots in the Cage5:00 The Yoel Romero Example6:30 The Three Ways to Entertain Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Masvidal Never Answered the Question
articles.description: Jorge Masvidal responded to Chael. But he didn’t answer the question. After Chael said Masvidal isn’t returning to the UFC, Masvidal fired back — hard. But in all of it, Chael points out one key detail that never got addressed. He never said he’s coming back. In this fan-driven segment, Chael breaks down the response and why it left out the only thing that actually mattered. Because if Masvidal really believed he was returning, there was a simple way to say it. And he didn’t. Now the question becomes why. Was it intentional? Was it uncertainty? Or something else entirely? Because when a fighter pushes back that hard — and still avoids the one claim at the center of it, it tells you more than anything he actually said. This isn’t about who won the exchange. It’s about what didn’t get said. #UFC #MMA #JorgeMasvidal #ChaelSonnenBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chael Predicts Next UFC Heavyweight Champion...It's Not Who You Think!
articles.description: Chael Sonnen Predicts the Next UFC Heavyweight Champion - And It's Not Who You Think Chael Sonnen answers your questions in another episode of Ask Uncle Chael. This week Chael makes a bold prediction on who holds the UFC Heavyweight Championship at the end of 2026, breaks down why Tom Aspinall hiring Eddie Hearn could be a serious psychological problem heading into his next fight, and explains why Jon Jones has no business being outside the top 15 regardless of his retirement status. Plus Chael shares what keeps his passion and energy burning after all these years in combat sports, weighs in on the Bisping vs Hamill controversy, and debates who has had the most successful post-UFC career. Whether you're a die-hard MMA fan or just discovering Chael Sonnen, this is the episode that reminds you why Uncle Chael is the most informed voice in the fight game. Subscribe and hit the bell so you never miss an episode. 00:00 — Introduction: Ask Uncle Chael begins 00:33 — Who holds the UFC Heavyweight belt at end of 2026? 00:58 — Aspinall hiring Eddie Hearn and why it's a psychological problem 01:57 — Alex Pereira gets first crack at the heavyweight title 02:58 — Did Bisping intentionally injure Matt Hamill's arm? 03:47 — Is Chael turning his home into an MMA training camp? 04:29 — How Chael keeps his energy and passion for combat sports 05:06 — Why knowing you're the smartest guy in the room never gets old 05:41 — Has the UFC sucked since moving to Paramount? 06:16 — Chael's take on AI in sports and entertainment 07:07 — Brendan Schaub vs Tito Ortiz: Who was the worse press secretary? 07:35 — Who has had the most successful post-UFC career? 08:29 — How close is too close? Chael on personal space and self-defense 09:16 — The June 6th Apex card — what do we know? 09:33 — How many times will Jon Jones retire and unretire in 2026? 10:04 — Why Jon Jones shouldn't be outside the top 15 10:52 — USADA testing pool question nobody's forced the UFC to answer Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: This Advice Gets Gaethje Knocked Out.
articles.description: Roy Jones Jr. wants to help Justin Gaethje prepare for Ilia Topuria. But Chael thinks that could be a big mistake. Because what works in boxing doesn’t always translate to MMA — and in this case, it might do more harm than good. Roy’s advice focuses on defense, slipping punches, and taking some of the sting off incoming shots. That works in boxing. In MMA, it can get you knocked out. Chael breaks down the difference between the two sports and why relying on defensive boxing tactics with four-ounce gloves is a dangerous strategy. Especially against a puncher like Ilia Topuria. Plus, the bigger question: Can a fighter at Gaethje’s level actually learn something new just weeks before a fight and use it effectively? Or is everything he’s going to rely on already built years ago? Because when it comes to fight night… there’s no time to experiment. #UFC #MMA #JustinGaethje #IliaTopuria #RoyJonesJr #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Roy’s Offer 0:25 The Real Question 1:10 Can You Learn This Late? 2:10 Why It Works in Boxing 3:10 Why It Fails in MMA 4:20 The Real Risk Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor Is Only Fighting Once More
articles.description: Conor McGregor is coming back. That part feels certain. But what happens after that? Chael breaks down the real question surrounding Conor’s return, not whether he fights again, but whether he fights more than once. And when you actually look at the situation, it gets complicated. Because no matter what happens in that first fight, it’s hard to find a path to a second one. If he wins, does he risk it all again? If he loses, can the UFC realistically sell him in another main event? Add in the time away, the injury, the weight class, and the lack of clear stakes… and suddenly, the idea of a second fight becomes very hard to justify. So while a return feels likely…a second one might not be. Because sometimes, the comeback fight isn’t the beginning. It’s the end. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “UFC Only Promotes UFC!” Aljo And DJ On UFC Promotion
The Most Dangerous Fighter Isn’t Broke #ufc
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan isn’t fighting for money—and that’s exactly what makes him so dangerous. In this clip, the mindset behind Tsarukyan’s rise is broken down. While many fighters are chasing paychecks, fame, or opportunities outside the cage, Tsarukyan is focused on one thing: becoming a world champion. No distractions. No shortcuts. Just constant improvement—whether that’s wrestling, jiu-jitsu, or sharpening his overall game. Here’s the truth about combat sports—when money is no longer the motivation, the fighter becomes unpredictable in the best way. There’s no hesitation, no safety net thinking. Just pure competition. And historically, those are the fighters nobody wants to deal with. It challenges a common belief too. People assume hunger comes from struggle—but sometimes, the most dangerous athlete is the one who doesn’t need anything except the win. That’s the difference between fighting for survival… and fighting for greatness. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ArmanTsarukyan #UFC #MMA #FightMindset #UFCLightweight #MMANews #CombatSportsshorts

articles.title: Who Was Using Jon Jones as Leverage?
articles.description: Francis Ngannou says the Jon Jones fight was never going to happen. But that raises a bigger question. If it wasn’t real… who was using who? Chael breaks down the situation and why this doesn’t add up at face value. Because at the time, everything pointed toward Jon Jones moving to heavyweight specifically to fight Francis. The plan seemed clear. The fight seemed inevitable. So if it was all just negotiation… where did that come from? Was Francis using Jon Jones as leverage to get a better deal? Or was the UFC using Jon Jones as a way to keep Francis in line? And if it was never real… why is Francis still saying it’s the fight he wants now? This isn’t just about a missed matchup. It’s about what was actually happening behind the scenes — and which side was playing the game. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Francis’ Statement 0:20 The Real Question 1:00 Was the UFC Bluffing? 2:10 Was Francis Bluffing? 3:30 Why This Doesn’t Add Up Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Brock vs Ngannou? There’s a Price #ufc
articles.description: Brock Lesnar vs Francis Ngannou might sound impossible—but in this game, there’s always a number. In this clip, the reality of “retirement” in combat sports gets broken down. When fighters say they’re done, it often means one thing—there’s no opportunity big enough on the table. But change the number, change the stage, and suddenly everything is back in play. A potential Netflix mega-fight? A payday in the $25 million range? That’s the kind of scenario that could bring Lesnar out of retirement. And let’s be clear—this isn’t about legacy. Lesnar’s already a former UFC heavyweight champion and a clear Hall of Fame-caliber name, a point even Paul Heyman has defended publicly. The real question is simple: how does Lesnar see himself right now? Is he truly done… or just waiting for the right moment? Because if the opportunity shows up—big enough, loud enough, and lucrative enough—don’t be surprised if one of the biggest stars in combat sports history walks back into the spotlight. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and fight business insights. #BrockLesnar #FrancisNgannou #UFC #MMA #FightBusiness #NetflixFight #CombatSports

articles.title: Did Brock Lesnar Have the Best Heavyweight Career?
articles.description: Who had the best heavyweight career in the UFC? Jon Jones Francis Ngannou Brock Lesnar Most people immediately go to skill, dominance, or championships. But what if that’s not the right way to look at it? Chael breaks down a different way to judge greatness not just by what happened in the cage, but by the total career. How often you fought. How much damage you took. And yes… how much money you made. Because if you look at it that way, the answer might surprise you. Brock Lesnar became champion, fought the biggest names of his era, and did it all in fewer fights while making more money than anyone else in the division. So what really defines the “best” career? Skill? Accomplishments? Longevity? Or something else entirely? #UFC #MMA #BrockLesnar #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Question 0:25 How Do You Define “Best”? 1:20 Jon vs Francis Problem 2:40 Brock’s Case 4:00 The Money Argument 6:10 Final ThoughtBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “Survive Me” for $200K—Seriously?#ufc
articles.description: Kyle Snyder just got a wild offer—and it comes from Khamzat Chimaev. In this clip, the $200,000 challenge gets broken down. Chimaev says: come spar, survive, and the money’s yours. Sounds simple… but what does “survive” actually mean? Is it a full sparring session? A brutal conditioning test? Or just making it through one of the toughest workouts in MMA? That’s where things get interesting. Chimaev is known for relentless training—sprints, cardio, grappling, nonstop pressure. But Snyder isn’t just anyone. He’s an Olympic-level athlete with elite conditioning and toughness. Getting through a workout? That’s not the question. The question is: what exactly are the rules? Because there’s a big difference between surviving a training session… and surviving a fight. This isn’t just a challenge—it’s a clash between two worlds: elite wrestling and elite MMA. And depending on how “survive” is defined, this could be anything from a showcase… to absolute chaos. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #KyleSnyder #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #Wrestling #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Brock Would Fight Ngannou
articles.description: Brock Lesnar says he’s retired. But what does that actually mean? Chael breaks down why retirement in combat sports doesn’t always mean the end — and why it often comes down to opportunity, not desire. Because when the right offer comes along… things change. And in Brock’s case, there may be one fight that changes everything. Francis Ngannou. From how Brock views his own career to why fighters really walk away, this isn’t just about retirement — it’s about what would bring someone back. Because sometimes… “done” doesn’t mean done. #MMA #UFC #BrockLesnar #FrancisNgannou #ChaelSonnen Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: McGregor vs Holloway? I Don’t Buy It #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway sounds big—but does it actually make sense? In this clip, the matchup gets put under the microscope. Yes, they’ve fought before—but that was years ago at 145 pounds, a division neither man currently competes in. So how much does that history really matter today? The bigger issue is weight and context. If McGregor is returning at 170, why match him with someone who isn’t a natural welterweight? If the goal is to create a “special night,” shouldn’t it involve fighters who actually live in that division? There’s no denying the appeal. Both names carry weight. Both would show up. And the fight would generate massive attention. But when you dig deeper, it starts to feel like a matchup built on nostalgia rather than logic. This is the difference between a big fight and the right fight. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #UFCReturn #MMANews

articles.title: Conor McGregor's 3 Impossible Problems...
articles.description: Conor's 3 Impossible Problems Conor McGregor isn’t just coming back. He’s trying to do something nobody does. Chael breaks down the three biggest problems working against Conor right now — and why each one on its own would already be a massive obstacle. Six years away from competition. A catastrophic injury. And a move to a new weight class. Most fighters can’t overcome one of those. Conor has to overcome all three. So what does that mean for his return? And why does the opponent almost not matter? Because this isn’t about who he fights. It’s about whether a comeback like this is even possible.
